concept: Lazy / Unwilling to Work / Avoiding Effort
description: Unwilling to work or use energy; avoiding exertion. 'Wesha' names the quality of laziness—the reluctance to engage effort, the preference for ease over activity.
sound_symbolism: The word slumps. 'We' begins with soft opening, energy low. Then 'shao' /ʃa/ exhales without force, effort avoided.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'wesha miona' (lazy person). Distinct from 'shorui' (weary) which names legitimate tiredness. Laziness is choice, weariness is state.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Laziness threatened survival—communities depended on each member's contribution.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sloth (thina) is a hindrance—but sometimes what appears lazy is needed rest, requiring discernment.
